element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation mechanic carried from Mega Man X; player assumes competence
Core offensive tool; charging mechanic central to combat rhythm
Each boss awards unique weapon; order flexibility allows difficulty scaling

element_introduction_order.element: Maverick weapons (Silk Shot, Speed Burner, etc.)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Strategic tool selection; some weapons effective against specific bosses/stages
Optional progression; incentivizes exploration and stage replay
Encounters tied to Zero body-part collection; narrative tension device
Optional collection; affects ending branching and final boss composition

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Specific stages (Bubble Splash, Magnet Mine referenced)

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Escalating threat; narrative revelation of true form elevates challenge

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical: Player-controlled stage selection allows tension management; each stage escalates toward boss encounter, then resets on stage clear. X-Hunter encounters intersperse between Maverick battles, building narrative tension toward final confrontation.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden armor pieces and weapon upgrades placed in platforming sections requiring exploration beyond critical path; Maverick weapon availability signals boss-defeat progression
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden paths accessible via dash jumps, wall-climbing, and ability combinations. Rewards optional power-ups and collectibles (Zero parts).

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle entry via optional stage-select order; player can choose preferred difficulty sequence among eight Mavericks. Baseline assumes Mega Man X competency.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating complexity as player accumulates weapons and armor; X-Hunter encounters introduce mandatory combat challenges; encourages tactical weapon selection and boss-order strategy
difficulty_curve.late_game: X-Hunter fortress and Sigma encounters force full mastery of mechanics; branching ending based on Zero parts collection adds replayability and strategic incentive
notable_spikes: First mandatory X-Hunter encounter (breaks stage autonomy)
Sigma final form as computer virus (thematic escalation)

notable_level_patterns.description: Eight selectable stages, each themed around a Maverick boss. Side-scrolling progression with platforming obstacles and mid-boss encounters building toward final boss. Hidden armor and weapon upgrades reward exploration.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Wheel Gator's stage (contains hidden X-Buster upgrade)

notable_level_patterns.description: X-Hunters appear among standard Maverick stages to claim Zero body parts, inserting narrative interruptions into otherwise autonomous stage-clear loop. Creates asymmetrical pacing.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any of eight stages (Serges, Agile, or Violen may appear)

notable_level_patterns.description: Final stage-cluster after eight Mavericks defeated. X-Hunter stronghold followed by Central Computer/Sigma confrontation. Branching structure based on Zero parts collection.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: X-Hunter fortress → Central Computer (conditional boss: Zero clone vs. Sigma alone)

notable_level_patterns.description: Defeating Mavericks grants stage-specific weapons effective against other bosses, creating strategic dependency and incentive for non-linear stage sequencing. Encourages experimentation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Spiral progression: discover weapon effectiveness, apply to remaining bosses

sources_used: wikipedia - Mega Man X2 (gameplay section, plot structure)
tcrf - Mega Man X2 (unused objects, regional differences for narrative context)
